title: artanthe nitidula
intro: Artanthe nitidula, also known as the red milkwood, is a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of snake bites and other venomous injuries, due to its ability to neutralize the effects of venom. The health benefits of Artanthe nitidula include its antivenom properties, as well as its potential to aid in the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. In herbal preparations, Artanthe nitidula is often used to make tinctures, decoctions, and infusions, which can be applied topically or taken internally to harness its medicinal properties.
